title: Intensely Garlicky Syrian Eggplant Stew
description: A comforting and flavorful Syrian stew featuring roasted eggplant, intense garlic, and a blend of aromatic spices, including shichimi-togarashi, finished with a tangy nuka-pickle garnish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the eggplant into 1-inch cubes and roast in the oven for 30 minutes, or until tender.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the shichimi-togarashi and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the roasted eggplant, tomato paste, and vegetable broth to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the stew for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then garnish with chopped nuka-pickle.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional nuka-pickle if desired.
